body.title: Move log parsing out of Igor — to Librarian (inference) or Scraps (deterministic)
body.result: DailyConsoleFileHandler: force_terminal=True → no_color=True; ANSI codes no longer written to .console.log files
body.description:
Igor currently contains log-parsing and ANSI-escape-stripping code that belongs outside cognition. Architecture principle: Igor uses systems, does not contain them (see memory). Log parsing with inference → Librarian MCP tool. Deterministic log scraping → Scraps device. Igor calls the tool, does not implement it.

Discovery step: grep devices/igor/ for ANSI stripping, log parsing, console scraping. Map each to Librarian vs Scraps based on whether inference is needed.

Context: console log ANSI escape codes are appearing raw in Kate editor because tmux adds them; the fix is to strip at source (in the log producer) or handle in the consuming tool, not in Igor cognition.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step required. Likely in devices/igor/web/ or devices/igor/cognition/. Also check devices/web_server/ for any log parsing that leaked in.
**Design rules:** igor-uses-not-contains (memory), Compiled_Inference.md framework (lab/docs/Compiled_Inference.md)
**Scope boundary:** IN — find log-processing code in Igor; relocate to Librarian/Scraps; wire Igor to call the tool; OUT — do not redesign Librarian or Scraps architecture; do not change log format or output
**Test plan:** grep -rn 'ANSI\|strip_escape\|ansi_escape\|log_parse' devices/igor/ returns 0 hits after fix; Igor log reading behavior unchanged via tool call; pytest passes
